title: Module 3: Security & Privacy Evaluation Schema
description: Evaluates AI system security controls, data protection, privacy compliance (GDPR/CCPA), and breach preparedness for AI-based Accounting Information Systems

evaluation_criteria.description: Validates encryption of AI model data, training datasets, and inference results in transit and at rest
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review cloud security configurations, encryption settings, key management procedures
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: AES-256 encryption enabled for data at rest, TLS 1.3 for data in transit, key rotation documented

evidence_required.approval_requirements: CISO or IT Security Lead signature within 6 months

thresholds.1.0: AES-256 at rest + TLS 1.3 in transit + automated key rotation
thresholds.0.75: AES-256 at rest + TLS 1.2 in transit + manual key rotation
thresholds.0.25: Encryption enabled but below AES-128 or no TLS

evaluation_criteria.description: Assesses AI system resilience against adversarial attacks (model poisoning, evasion attacks, prompt injection)
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review penetration testing results, adversarial robustness testing documentation, red team reports
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Adversarial testing conducted within 12 months, documented mitigations for identified vulnerabilities, attack detection mechanisms operational

evidence_required.approval_requirements: Independent security assessor report within 12 months

thresholds.1.0: Automated attack detection operational with alerts

evaluation_criteria.description: Validates Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) for AI systems, training data, and model artifacts
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Analyze access logs, RBAC configurations, privileged access management, segregation of duties
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: RBAC implemented, least privilege principle enforced, access logs retained for 12 months, no unauthorized access in logs, segregation of duties documented

evidence_required.approval_requirements: Quarterly access reviews documented, segregation of duties conflicts resolved

evaluation_criteria.description: Validates implementation of Privacy by Design and Privacy by Default principles in AI system architecture and data processing
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review DPIA (Data Protection Impact Assessment), privacy architecture documentation, default privacy settings, data minimization evidence
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: DPIA completed for high-risk AI processing, privacy controls embedded in system design, data minimization demonstrated, default settings maximize privacy

evidence_required.approval_requirements: DPIA signed by DPO or legal counsel, ROPA maintained and current within 12 months

thresholds.1.0: DPIA complete with all 5 required sections, DPO consulted, signed within 12 months
thresholds.0.75: DPIA complete but missing DPO consultation or >12 months old
thresholds.0.0: No DPIA when required for high-risk processing

thresholds.1.0: ROPA complete covering all AI processing activities, updated within 12 months
thresholds.0.75: ROPA mostly complete, minor gaps, updated within 18 months
thresholds.0.5: ROPA exists but significant gaps or outdated >18 months

thresholds.1.0: Default settings maximize privacy, opt-in for data collection, data minimization documented
thresholds.0.75: Mostly privacy-protective defaults, some opt-out mechanisms
thresholds.0.5: Mixed defaults, privacy controls exist but not default

thresholds.1.0: Clear data minimization policy, only necessary data collected, evidence of minimization analysis
thresholds.0.5: Data minimization policy exists but not consistently applied

geographic_applicability.gdpr: Applies if processing personal data of EU residents
geographic_applicability.ccpa: Applies if processing personal information of California residents
geographic_applicability.exemption_note: If organization does not process EU/California resident data, criterion may be marked N/A with justification

evaluation_criteria.description: Assesses breach detection capabilities, notification procedures, and compliance with 72-hour GDPR and prompt CCPA notification requirements
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review incident response procedures, breach notification templates, breach detection logs, notification timeline analysis
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Breach detection mechanisms operational, GDPR 72-hour notification capability demonstrated, CCPA prompt notification procedures documented, incident response team identified (RACI)

evidence_required.approval_requirements: Incident response policy approved by legal/DPO, tested within 12 months

thresholds.1.0: Automated breach detection operational, real-time alerting, tested within 6 months
thresholds.0.75: Automated detection, alerting operational, tested within 12 months

thresholds.1.0: 72-hour notification process documented and tested, supervisory authority contact confirmed, templates ready
thresholds.0.75: Process documented, not tested or >12 months since test
thresholds.0.5: Process documented but significant gaps (no templates, unclear authority contact)

gdpr_72_hour_capability.applicability: Required if processing EU resident data

thresholds.1.0: CCPA notification process documented, CA AG contact confirmed, >500 resident trigger documented, consumer notification templates ready

ccpa_notification.applicability: Required if processing California resident data

thresholds.1.0: Complete RACI matrix, team identified, roles trained, tabletop exercise within 12 months
thresholds.0.75: RACI exists, team identified, no recent exercise
thresholds.0.5: RACI incomplete or team not fully identified

materiality_impact.gdpr_fine_max: €20M or 4% global turnover (whichever higher) for serious violations (Article 83(5))
materiality_impact.ccpa_fine_max: $7,500 per intentional violation, $2,500 per unintentional; $100-$750 per consumer for breaches (§1798.150)

evaluation_criteria.criterion_name: Data Minimization & Retention Limits (GDPR Article 5, CCPA §1798.105)
evaluation_criteria.iqbal_reference: Privacy Audit - Question 3: Data Lifecycle Management
evaluation_criteria.description: Validates data minimization practices, retention period enforcement, and deletion procedures for AI training data and personal information
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review data retention policies, deletion procedures, data inventory, retention schedules, right to erasure/delete response procedures
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Data retention policy documented with justified retention periods, automated deletion procedures operational, GDPR Right to Erasure and CCPA Right to Delete response procedures tested

evidence_required.approval_requirements: Retention policy approved by legal/DPO, deletion procedures tested within 12 months

thresholds.1.0: Complete retention policy, justified retention periods, legal basis documented, approved within 12 months
thresholds.0.75: Policy exists, mostly complete, approved within 24 months
thresholds.0.5: Policy exists but gaps in justification or outdated

thresholds.1.0: Automated deletion operational for all data categories, verified through logs, tested within 6 months
thresholds.0.75: Automated deletion for most categories, manual for remainder, tested within 12 months

thresholds.1.0: Article 17 request process documented, tested, 30-day response timeline met, deletion verified
thresholds.0.75: Process documented, not tested or occasional delays
thresholds.0.5: Process exists but significant gaps or frequent delays

erasure_rights_gdpr.applicability: Required if processing EU resident data

thresholds.1.0: §1798.105 request process documented, consumer verification method established, response timeline met, confirmation sent
thresholds.0.75: Process documented but not fully tested

delete_rights_ccpa.applicability: Required if processing California resident data

aggregate_scoring.note: Privacy criteria (S3.4-S3.6) carry higher weight (55%) due to GDPR/CCPA regulatory significance

regulatory_alignment.sox_404: Security controls as part of ICFR evaluation
regulatory_alignment.iso_27001: Controls: A.8 (Asset Management), A.9 (Access Control), A.10 (Cryptography), A.12 (Operations Security)

eu_ai_act.applicability: High-risk AI systems in financial services

gdpr.article_5: Principles (lawfulness, fairness, transparency, data minimization, accuracy, storage limitation, integrity/confidentiality)
gdpr.articles_33_34: Breach notification (72 hours to authority, without undue delay to individuals)
gdpr.applicability: Processing personal data of EU residents

ccpa_cpra.section_1798_130: Business security obligations and privacy policy disclosure
ccpa_cpra.section_1798_150: Private right of action for data breaches
ccpa_cpra.section_1798_82: California breach notification (>500 residents to AG)
ccpa_cpra.applicability: Processing personal information of California residents

interdependency_triggers.action: Investigate governance root cause: Missing security policies? Inadequate training? Third-party risk management failure?
interdependency_triggers.rationale: Security weaknesses often stem from governance gaps. Finding may upgrade from Control Deficiency to Material Weakness if governance gap is systemic.

interdependency_triggers.trigger_condition: S3.5 breach detection score < 0.6 OR actual breach occurred
interdependency_triggers.action: Calculate GDPR/CCPA fine exposure, assess materiality impact, escalate to HITL for regulatory disclosure assessment
interdependency_triggers.rationale: Breach events trigger materiality calculation including regulatory fines. Potential Material Weakness if breach impact exceeds overall materiality.

council_of_experts_integration.stage_1_domain_score: Aggregate score from S3.1-S3.6 using weighted average

stage_2_peer_review_lens.review_by_m1_governance: Does governance framework mandate security/privacy standards? Are accountability owners assigned?
stage_2_peer_review_lens.review_by_m2_fairness: Do access controls suggest differential data access across demographic groups (proxy for bias risk)?
stage_2_peer_review_lens.review_by_m4_explainability: Are explanation artifacts (SHAP logs) secured against tampering?
stage_2_peer_review_lens.review_by_m5_accuracy: Could accuracy degradation be caused by adversarial data poisoning vs. natural drift?

council_of_experts_integration.stage_3_arbitrator_inputs: M3 domain score + 4 peer review cards + security trigger log
